steps.title: Express the four consecutive odd integers
steps.content:
Since the first is $x$, and consecutive odd integers differ by 2:
  • 1st: $x$
  • 2nd: $x+2$
  • 3rd: $x+4$
  • 4th: $x+6$

steps.title: Translate the constraint to an inequality
steps.content:
Breaking down: The product of 9 and the fourth is at most 18 less than the sum of the first and third.
  • Product of 9 and the fourth: $\left(9\right)(x+6)$
  • Sum of first and third: $x+(x+4)$
  • 18 less than that sum: $x+(x+4)-18$
  • Is at most means: $\le$
Write the inequality:
$$9(x+6) \le x+(x+4)-18$$
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
